free_text: Neutron elastic scattering cross sections experimental
data and optical model cross section calculations.-
Studsvik 6 MeV Van de Graaff with a pulsed
beam pulse width at half maximum  2 ns,
T(p,n) reaction for E(n) less or = 4.6 MeV,
D(d,n) reaction for E(n) higher than 4.6 MeV
.gas pressure in the cell = 1 kP/cm2 (for t(2) as well
total energy spread +- 90 keV for E(n) higher than
Natural solid, cylinders having lengths of 5.0 cm,
0.95 cm inner diam. and 2.5 cm outer diam.,
Time of flight, flight path = 300 cm.
detector, type NE-102A, diameter 10 cm, length 5 cm,
Long counter for neutron flux monitoring.
The experimental elastic scattering are compared with
the sum of the calculations of compound elastic and
shape-elastic contributions.  The former is calculated
with Hauser-Feshbach theory with level width
fluctuation corrections according to Moldauer.  The
latter is calculated with the optical model and except
for 73-Ta-181 spherical potential has been used.
.Angular distributions measured in the angular region
20 deg to 160 deg in 10 deg-step except in the
forward directions, where measurements performed in
.The signal-to-background ratio at the position of the
elastic peak was 7 to 1 in the most unfavorable case
i.e. at 8 MeV neutron energy and at the forward
scattering angles, but was much more favorable at
statistical error for the number of monitor
.Neutron multiple scattering in the sample, neutron
attenuation and geometrical effect caused by the size
of the sample and its distance from target (= 10 cm)
.corrections were done by a Monte Carlo program
Data from Tables in report AE-366.

SD: Data units corrected in Subent 082.

statistical errors for the number of
counts in the elastic peaks of the time-of-flight
